description:
This shape is identical to the one found in Rex's problem 3041.  This one asks you to think about it without the possibility of ko.  

Thanks Blyx!
sgf:
(;C[Assuming neither side has ko threats and it is black's turn, what is the status of the white group?  Is it unconditionally (A)live?  In (S)eki?  (D)ead?FORCE]AB[pa]AB[oa]AB[ob]AB[oc]AB[pc]AW[pb]AW[qb]AW[qa]AW[qc]AW[rd]AW[re]AW[rf]AW[sf]AW[qf]AB[qd]AB[pd]AB[qe]AB[pf]AB[pg]AB[qg]AB[rg]AB[sg]AB[jg]LB[jd:A]LB[ld:S]LB[nd:D]AP[goproblems]
(;B[jd]C[No, please navigate to see why.])
(;B[nd]C[No, please navigate to see why.])
(;B[ld];W[le]C[Prove it!]
(;B[sc];W[sb]
(;B[rb];W[rc];B[sa];W[sd])
(;B[rc];W[rb]))
(;B[rc];W[rb];B[sc];W[sb])
(;B[rb];W[rc]
(;B[sc];W[sd];B[sa];W[sb]C[Black has no ko threats with which to fight this.])
(;B[sb];W[sc])
(;B[ra];W[sb]))
(;B[sb]
(;W[sc]C[CHOICE]
(;B[sd];W[rc]C[White has no ko threats, so S18 is not possible.];B[ra]C[Great job!RIGHT])
(;B[ra];W[rc]
(;B[sd]C[Great job!RIGHT])
(;B[se]C[RIGHT]))
(;B[rb];W[rc]))
(;W[rc]C[CHOICE]
(;B[sc]
(;W[sd]C[CHOICE];B[ra]C[Great job!RIGHT])
(;W[rb]C[CHOICE];B[sd]C[Great job!  White has no ko threats, so T19 is not possible.RIGHT]))
(;B[ra];W[rb])
(;B[rb];W[sc])
(;B[sd]
(;W[rb]C[CHOICE];B[sc]C[Great job!  Without ko threats, white cannot play T19.RIGHT])
(;W[sc]C[CHOICE];B[ra]C[Great job!RIGHT])))
(;W[rb]C[CHOICE]
(;B[sd];W[rc]C[With no ko threats, white cannot play T17.];B[sc]C[Great job!  White has no ko threats, so T19 is not possible.RIGHT])
(;B[sc];W[sd];B[ra];W[sa]C[Black has no ko threats.])))

(;W[sc]C[CHOICE];B[sb];W[rc];B[ra]C[Great job!RIGHT])
(;W[rb]C[CHOICE];B[sb];W[rc]C[White cannot play T17 due to lack of ko threats.];B[sc]C[Great job!  Since white has no ko threats, T19 is not possible.RIGHT])
(;W[rc]C[CHOICE]
(;B[sb]
(;W[sc]C[CHOICE];B[ra]C[Great job!RIGHT])
(;W[rb]C[CHOICE];B[sc]C[Great job!  White cannot play T19 due to a lack of ko threats.RIGHT]))
